name: Schisandra (Schisandra chinensis)
summary: Schisandra (Schisandra chinensis) is a five-flavor berry used in TCM as an adaptogen and liver tonic. Its primary lignans (schisandrin A/B/C) support hepatocyte detoxification via CYP450 modulation and exhibit antioxidant activity. Human data support mild stress reduction and liver enzyme normalization in hepatitis populations.

name: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum)
summary: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is a yellow-flowered herb with well-studied antidepressant effects for mild-to-moderate depression. Its active constituents (hypericin, hyperforin) inhibit reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to low-dose SSRIs for mild depression, with fewer side effects in that population.

name: Turkey Tail
summary: Turkey Tail (Trametes versicolor) is a shelf mushroom yielding two extensively studied polysaccharopeptides: PSK (krestin) and PSP. These compounds stimulate NK cells, macrophages, and T-cell activity, and PSK is approved in Japan as an adjunct cancer immunotherapy. Human evidence for standalone immune support in healthy populations is limited but supportive.
